byAlton Worley II, KOMO News

PORT ANGELES, Wash. —A hiker was rescued by a United States Coast Guard (USCG) aircrew after adrift logs injured him late Saturday night.

The rising tide caused logs to float adrift and injure the 44-year-old man near themouth of the Hoh River. USCG said two others in his party assisted the man before the aircrew from Port Angeles.



The aircrew brought the man to the air station where he received further aid before being taken to Olympic Medical Center.

USCG said the man's current condition is unknown.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Coast Guard aircrew rescues hiker after adrift logs injure him
description: A hiker was rescued by an United States Coast Guard (USCG) aircrew after adrift logs injured him late Saturday night.
